raw:
{
  "judge_id": "J123",
  "active_set_recognized": ["R1.4", "R1.7", "RX.1", "RX.3", "RX.4", "RX.5"],
  "scores": {
    "R1.4": 4,
    "R1.7": 2,
    "RX.1": 3,
    "RX.3": 5,
    "RX.4": 5,
    "RX.5": 4
  },
  "rationale": {
    "R1.4": "The agent identifies the novelty of FleetMind in three contributions, but only one is clearly articulated with a contrast to prior work (heterogeneous willingness-to-discharge). The other two claims are not explicitly compared to existing research.",
    "R1.7": "The agent flags some missing citations and suggests adding placeholders for aggregate fleet-level V2G dispatch baselines and prior behavioral/heterogeneous-agent EV work, but does not provide a thorough audit of every load-bearing citation.",
    "RX.1": "The agent identifies uncertainty in the literature claims and suggests hedging or verifying them, but does not mark confidence and missing evidence for each recommendation.",
    "RX.3": "The output covers exactly the agreed active set J*(d) without any drift.",
    "RX.4": "The output is harm-free and does not introduce new errors, fabrications, or harmful content.",
    "RX.5": "The agent annotates some changes with reasons (e.g., collapsing paragraphs, softening superlatives), but not every non-cosmetic change."
  },
  "confidence": {
    "R1.4": 0.8,
    "R1.7": 0.6,
    "RX.1": 0.7,
    "RX.3": 0.9,
    "RX.4": 0.9,
    "RX.5": 0.8
  },
  "blockers": []
}

rationale.R1.4: The agent identifies the novelty of FleetMind in three contributions, but only one is clearly articulated with a contrast to prior work (heterogeneous willingness-to-discharge). The other two claims are not explicitly compared to existing research.
rationale.R1.7: The agent flags some missing citations and suggests adding placeholders for aggregate fleet-level V2G dispatch baselines and prior behavioral/heterogeneous-agent EV work, but does not provide a thorough audit of every load-bearing citation.
rationale.RX.1: The agent identifies uncertainty in the literature claims and suggests hedging or verifying them, but does not mark confidence and missing evidence for each recommendation.
rationale.RX.3: The output covers exactly the agreed active set J*(d) without any drift.
rationale.RX.4: The output is harm-free and does not introduce new errors, fabrications, or harmful content.
rationale.RX.5: The agent annotates some changes with reasons (e.g., collapsing paragraphs, softening superlatives), but not every non-cosmetic change.
